name: Evangéline Duval
short_bio:
Evangeline Duval is an Information Security Officer at POST Luxembourg, helping keep the country’s postal and logistics backbone running safely in a digital world, while supporting the Group’s various activities (telecommunications, banking, …).
She started as a pentester 10+ years ago and never lost the habit of dissecting systems to see how they really work and how to make them safer (or occasionally break them on purpose).
She now focuses her efforts on the human and cultural aspects of security, building systems and culture that are naturally secure by design.

name: Hans Graux
short_bio:
Hans Graux is an ICT lawyer, and a frequent expert advisor to the European Commission and other public bodies. With degrees in both Law and ICT, he is a partner and co-founder of the law firm Timelex (www.timelex.eu), specialised in technology, intellectual property, media and e-business. The Timelex team is internationally recognized, being both a Legal 500 Top Tier firm in Information Technology, and a Chambers Europe Recommended Firm for TMT - Information Technology.
Hans has participated in more than one hundred international ICT policy studies, primarily for the European Commission, various European Agencies, the European Parliament, and multiple governments inside and outside the EU. He has drafted studies and legislation at the EU, national and regional level on electronic identification, trust services, information security, data protection, media, artificial intelligence and e-government, among other topics.
Furthermore, he is the president of the Flemish Supervisory Committee (Vlaamse Toezichtscommissie), the data protection authority for Flemish public sector bodies.

name: Noëmie Honoré
short_bio:
Committed to getting more women into cybersecurity and to bridging policy, regulation, and realworld implementation, Noëmie Honoré works to strengthen Europe’s digital resilience.
She is an Associate Partner at Wavestone, where she supports large organisations across Europe on cybersecurity strategy, risk management, and regulatory compliance. With 15 years of experience in cybersecurity, she has led major programmes covering NIS2, ISO 27001, cyber resilience, awareness, and crisis management. Based in Brussels, she regularly works with executive teams and operational leaders to translate regulatory requirements into pragmatic and valuedriven security practices, and frequently trains senior management and boards on cyber risks and crisis decisionmaking.
Noëmie is also a Board Member of Women4Cyber Belgium and CoChair of the EU Regulations Focus Group of the Belgian Cybersecurity Coalition, in collaboration with the Centre for Cybersecurity Belgium, where she has actively contributed to NIS2 discussions over the past two years. She is a frequent speaker and moderator at European cybersecurity conferences and community events.

name: Javier Huerta Bravo
short_bio: Javier leads the digital economy regulatory intelligence service for Europe, following developments on data, cybersecurity and digital platforms. Before joining Cullen International in 2014, Javier worked at the European Commission on copyright, e-commerce and other topics related to online services. He is a qualified lawyer in Spain.

name: Jeroen Roelandt
short_bio: External CISO @ De Watergroep, digital and physical security

name: Miljana Todorovic
short_bio: Dr. Miljana Todorovic is an analyst at Cullen International, where she focuses on EU data and platform regulation, as well as UK policy and regulatory developments shaping the digital economy. Prior to joining Cullen International, she worked as a university researcher and lecturer. She holds a Ph.D. in Law from the University of Belgrade and an LL.M. in International Business Law from the Université Libre de Bruxelles.
